findings.summary: AC-005 Postcondition 4 ingest-guard threshold off by the 8-byte HMAC tag — stated the router-side ingest guard rejects a raw datagram "shorter than 24 bytes", but the shipped wire layout ([8]HMACTag | [16]SVTNID | [8]NodeAddr | ...) and Decision 1/SEC-DW-01 both require 32 raw bytes (8-byte tag prefix + 24-byte key selector). A literal implementation would leave a 24-31-byte raw-datagram window that passes the length guard and then indexes out of bounds pre-authentication — CWE-770/400 class.
findings.remediation: story-writer — story v2.0→v2.1: AC-005 PC-4 corrected to "shorter than 32 bytes (8-byte HMAC tag + the 24-byte SVTNID/NodeAddr key selector) — equivalently, whose post-tag body is shorter than 24 bytes", plus an explanatory note on the body = post-tag-bytes convention and the 38-byte full-valid-frame minimum. Full-story sweep found AC-005 PC-4 was the sole occurrence. No AC added/removed (18 unchanged), points unchanged (8). input-hash unchanged (b4e0a5f, compute-input-hash --check confirmed no drift). STORY-INDEX v4.97→v4.98 (Backlog row + changelog row).

findings.summary: SEC-DW-07 state-lifetime asymmetry: the sender's in-memory Sequence counter resets to zero on node restart while the router's lastSeen watermark (keyed on the restart-stable admitted NodeAddr) persists across the restart. A legitimate re-admitted node's post-restart Sequence values fall behind the router's already-observed high-water mark and are silently discarded as apparent replay — locking the node out of discovery for up to ~8.3h (the existing wraparound bound), with no self-rescue since the cold-start admission path does not apply once a lastSeen entry already exists for the NodeAddr. Novel failure class relative to passes 1 and 3's byte-arithmetic findings: a design-level liveness gap in the anti-replay mechanism itself — the mirror-image failure to the replay threat SEC-DW-07/VP-080 already covered.
findings.remediation: architect — option (c) APPROVED: Sequence widened uint32→uint64, epoch-qualified (high 32 bits = uint32(time.Now().UTC().Unix()) sampled once at Discovery-instance start; low 32 bits = the existing in-memory counter). Router-side comparison logic unchanged — no new state, no new coupling to FO-(a). Two alternatives considered and rejected: (a) router-side lastSeen reset on reconnect — depends on a node-identity↔connection binding that does not exist in this design; (b) sender-side Sequence persistence across restart — introduces a new persistence class, weakest on the ephemeral compute this story targets. Wire consequences: hop-1 Sequence body[24:32] (was body[24:28]), count shifts to body[32:34], full-valid-frame minimum 38→42 bytes; hop-2 Sequence byte[12:20] (was byte[12:16]), count byte[20:22], fixed pre-session header 18→22 bytes. Artifact chain: rulings v1.4→v1.5→v1.6 (two-step — v1.5 landed the design amendment, v1.6 corrected a residual-bound imprecision the orchestrator caught before propagation, see below); VP-080 v1.1→v1.2→v1.3 (Property 4 rewritten for uint64 composite wraparound scoping, new Property 5 asserts restart forward-progress, input-hash c3d2fc5→fac2515→5e22481); VP-INDEX v2.41→v2.42→v2.43; BC-2.03.001 v1.5→v1.6 (PC-2 blockquote superseded to match the epoch-qualified field); story v2.2→v2.3 (five architect blockquotes applied — AC-008 scope-clarification note, AC-009 discard-rule characterization covering genuine replay plus two bounded restart residuals (EC-010), AC-010 PC-5 replaced + PC-6 added with new test TestVP080_DiscoveryIngest_RestartForwardProgress, new EC-010 edge-case row, Human Gate item 1 v1.6 sign-off paragraph appended; mechanical 38→42-byte sweep plus hop-2 re-transcription; uint32→uint64 sweep across Human Gate item 1, Non-Goals, and the File-Change List; full stale-BC-citation version-pin sweep amended in place to rulings v1.6/VP-080 v1.3; story input-hash c321c05→cd82f7b); STORY-INDEX v4.99→v4.100.

orchestrator_corrections.summary: Precision correction caught by the orchestrator BEFORE propagation, not itself a spec-adversarial-pass finding. VP-080 v1.2's Property 5 residual sentence (and the reproduced BC-2.03.001 PC-2 blockquote's trailing clause) conflated two distinct residual cases under a single "≤1 second" bound. They do not share a bound: the same-wall-clock-second crash-loop case IS correctly bounded to ≤1s, self-clearing at the next epoch tick; the backward-host-clock-adjustment case is instead bounded by the adjustment magnitude N — every advertisement is discarded (not just one) for a window of duration ≈N. Both remain accepted residuals — the crash-loop case because its bound is tight, the clock-adjustment case because its trigger (a restart coinciding with an operator clock misconfiguration) is rare, not because its duration is small.
orchestrator_corrections.disposition: FIXED — VP-080 v1.3 and rulings v1.6 split the two cases with independently stated bounds; the story's Human Gate item 1 ask now presents both bounds explicitly rather than one collapsed figure.

findings.summary: AC-004's advertisementKey deletion structurally broke the shipped VP-045 test that AC-007 mandated "passes unmodified" — rooted in rulings Implementation Constraint 3's false technical claim ("ReceiveAdvertisement preserved unchanged in shape"). Post-deletion, receive-side key derivation was unspecified: a node structurally cannot derive a foreign sender's DiscoveryAuthKey per BC-2.03.001 v1.6 Postcondition 5, and hop-2 relay frames carry zero HMACTag. Found by re-deriving the shipped advertisementKey call graph (3 call sites — Encode, Decode, ReceiveAdvertisement — not the 1 prior text-sweep passes assumed), a code-grounded layer prior passes could not reach through text sweeps alone.
findings.remediation: architect adjudication: Discovery.ReceiveAdvertisement RETIRED outright (deleted, not preserved — it cannot compile post-deletion since no key is derivable). Replaced by a new node-side relay-ingest function: decodes the hop-2 DISCOVERY_RELAY payload, no per-frame HMAC (trust = the admitted connection, AC-015), ErrSVTNMismatch relocated to a direct OuterHeader.SVTNID vs. d.cfg.LocalSVTNID equality check; same registry replace-on-write semantics. TestDiscovery_VP045_SVTNIsolation_MultipleScopes RETIRED outright (not extended) — its premise (node-local HMAC verification of advertisements) no longer has a subject once node-side HMAC verification is removed; equivalent SVTN-isolation coverage relocated to the router-side internal/discovery/discovery_wire_test.go (AC-005/AC-006). Ten ReceiveAdvertisement-calling tests enumerated for rewrite/retirement. IC-3 struck through with a pointer to the new "Node-local ingest correction" subsection. DI-004/SEC-DW-08/epoch-Sequence design untouched. BC-2.03.001 confirmed by architect to need NO amendment — its PC-5 rule is the very rule IC-3 contradicted. Artifact chain: rulings v1.7→v1.8 (closes_findings gains F-DWSP8-001); VP-045 v1.3→v1.4 (lifecycle citation correction only — narrowly revisits adjudication #8's non-update, sanctioned by orchestrator; PARTIAL status and the real-socket PC-3 gap unchanged); VP-INDEX v2.43→v2.44 (changelog row only); story v2.5→v2.6 (six architect blockquotes applied verbatim: AC-004 PC-5 qualifying note naming the three call sites, TD-031 symbol-only-citation deviation approved for the blockquote's line-number anchors, AC-007 full rewrite, File-Change List's ten-test row, Task 4 Red/Green rewrite, Decision 1's ReceiveAdvertisement bullet replaced, S-7.02 Previous Story Intelligence anchors-table row's trailing sentence replaced; mechanical rulings-version-pin sweep v1.7→v1.8 at five live spots; input-hash eccbdc4→a39b7ad since rulings is a declared story input); STORY-INDEX v4.102→v4.103 (Backlog row + changelog row).

findings.summary: Seventh instance of the family this cycle. Live prose attributed the F-DWSP4-001 restart-liveness amendment (`Sequence` `uint32`→`uint64` epoch-qualified widening + offset consequences) to "rulings v1.6", when the canonical adoption version is v1.5 — rulings.md's restart-liveness amendment section header reads "v1.5 adjudication"; v1.6 is only the residual-precision correction (zero width/offset changes). Adversary's anchors: AC-005's note, and Human Gate item 1's opening sentence — the latter directly contradicted line 1270's correct dual-version phrasing WITHIN the same Human Gate item, the text a human signs off on for SEC-DW-07. Adversary offered a downgrade-to-NITPICK path (streak would have advanced this pass); orchestrator DECLINED per grading precedent (F-DWSP11-001/F-DWSP12-001 both graded LOW for false-but-harmless claims, not downgraded) — expedience-over-integrity rejected.
findings.remediation: Fix-burst 11, story-writer only (orchestrator extended scope mid-burst) — story v2.10→v2.11. AC-005's note: "widths updated per F-DWSP4-001/rulings v1.6" → "...v1.5". Human Gate item 1's opening sentence: "epoch-qualified per the v1.6 restart-liveness amendment" → "...v1.5 restart-liveness amendment". A mandatory re-certification sweep after those two fixes surfaced a THIRD live spot making the identical false attribution — Human Gate item 1's fix-origin sentence, "The architect's fix (rulings v1.6): widen `Sequence` to `uint64`, epoch-qualified..." — the exact string prior sweeps' regex matched and MIS-SANCTIONED as a "point-in-time fix-origin citation" (passes 5-12 verified it unchanged without checking the semantic claim). Orchestrator extended the same burst to fix it rather than leave a guaranteed pass-14 finding (class-sweep principle). Fixed in place, same v2.11 (uncommitted, amended not re-versioned): → "The architect's fix (rulings v1.5; residual bounds corrected v1.6):" — the dual-version formula line 1270 already uses. All three same-class spots now fixed. Changelog records the exception-set retirement plus the going-forward convention (amendment attributions cite the ADOPTING version; dual formula where the correction also matters). Final sweep: zero live-prose rulings-v1.6 pins remain in any form; only remaining live sub-v1.8 rulings reference is the exempt Provenance bullet (v1.3); VP-080 uniformly v1.7. `input-hash` `a39b7ad` HOLDS (`--check` exit 0, multiple runs). STORY-INDEX v4.107→v4.108 (row 144 → draft (v2.11, 18 ACs, 8 pts); sub-narrative amended in place for the third spot; aggregates untouched).

process_gap.summary: Fourth sub-instance this cycle. The fixer's sweep exposed a THIRD same-class instance — Human Gate item 1's "The architect's fix (rulings v1.6): widen…" — which prior sweeps' regex matched and MIS-SANCTIONED as a "point-in-time fix-origin citation," verified unchanged burst after burst (passes 5-12) without checking the semantic claim. The erroneous sanctioned-exception classification is RETIRED: sanctioned-exception lists can entrench a false claim; classification now requires semantic verification, not just pattern presence. Strengthens (does not reopen) the recorded PG-DWSP6-01 process-gap — same S-7.02 cycle-close disposition item, no new entry created.
